resources:
SOLAPE Mercury ⑴ Mercury is the smallest Planet in our Solar System. 
 
 ⑵ It has the shortest orbit in the solar system. 
 
 ⑶ It has a thin atmosphere, which causes it to swing between burning and freezing temperatures. 
 
 ⑷ It is also a dense planet, composed mostly of iron and nickel with an iron core. 
 
 ⑸ It takes 88 days to travel around the Sun. 
 
 ⑹ It has an average distance of 58 million km to the Sun. 
 
 ⑺ It has 0 moons. 
 

⑴ Earth is the largest of the terrestrial planets. 
 
 ⑵ The Earth has formed approximately 4.54 billion years ago. 
 
 ⑶ It is the only known planet to support life. 
 
 ⑷ The Earth take 24 hours to rotate. 
 
 ⑸ It takes 365 days to travel around the Sun. 
 
 ⑹ It is an only Planet there are living beings. 
 
 ⑺ It has an average distance of 149.9 million km to the Sun. 
 
 ⑻ It has 1 moon. 
 

⑴ Venus is the hottest planet in the solar system. 
 
 ⑵ It has carbon dioxide in the atmosphere. 
 
 ⑶ There are extensive lava flows. 
 
 ⑷ Venus is about the same size as earth. 
 
 ⑸ It has an average distance of 108 million km to the Sun. 
 
 ⑹ It takes 255 days to travel around the Sun. 
 
 ⑺ It has 0 moons. 
 

⑴ Mars has the largest mountain in the solar system. 
 
 ⑵ It is known to have water ice and organics-some of the ingredients for living things. 
 
 ⑶ It is less dense than Earth and has a smaller magnetic. 
 
 ⑷ It has two small moons, Phobos and Deimos. 
 
 ⑸ It might have supported life 3.7 billion ago. 
 
 ⑹ It takes 687 Earth days to travel around the Sun. 
 
 ⑺ It has an average distance of 246.31 million km to the Sun. 
 
 ⑻ It has 2 moon. 
 

⑴ Jupiter is the fifth planet from the Sun and the largest in the solar system. 
 
 ⑵ It has a radius 11 times the size of the Earth. 
 
 ⑶ Its atmosphere consists mostly of hydrogen, helium, ammonia and methane. 
 
 ⑷ It is visible with the naked eye and was known by the ancients. 
 
 ⑸ It has an average distance of 778.5 million km to the Sun. 
 
 ⑹ It takes 4333 days to travel around the Sun. 
 
 ⑺ It has 53 confirmed and total 79 moons. 
 

⑴ Saturn is the sixth planet from the Sun and the second-largest in the solar system. 
 
 ⑵ It has a radius of nine and a half times the Earth. 
 
 ⑶ It is a gas-giant planet and therefore does not have a solid surface like Earth. 
 
 ⑷ It is over 95 times more massive. 
 
 ⑸ It has an average distance of 1.434 billion km to the Sun. 
 
 ⑹ It takes 10,759 days to travel around the Sun. 
 
 ⑺ It has 53 confirmed and total 82 moons. 
 

⑴ Uranus is the seventh planet from the Sun. 
 
 ⑵ It has the third-largest planetary radius and fourth-largest planetary mass in the solar system. 
 
 ⑶ It has a ring system, a magnetosphere, and numerous moons. 
 
 ⑷ It has an average distance of 2.871 billion km to the Sun. 
 
 ⑸ It takes 30,687 days to travel around the Sun. 
 
 ⑹ It has 27 moons. 
 

⑴ Neptune is the eighth and farthest known Solar planet from the Sun. 
 
 ⑵ In the Solar System, it is the fourth-largest planet by diameter, the third-most-massive planet and the densest giant planet. 
 
 ⑶ It is 17 times the mass of Earth, slightly more massive than its near-twin Uranus. 
 
 ⑷ It has an average distance of 4.495 billion km to the Sun. 
 
 ⑸ It takes 60,190 days to travel around the Sun. 
 
 ⑹ It has 14 moons. 
 

⑴ The sun is 4.6 billion years old. 
 
 ⑵ The sun has a diameter of 1,392,684 km and an equatorial distance of 4,370,005.6 km. 
 
 ⑶ The Sun is as bright as 4 trillion 100-watt light bulbs. Imagine paying for that energy bill! 
 
 ⑷ The Sunlight takes 8 minutes to reach Earth. 
 
 ⑸ The Sun rotates on its axis once in about 27 days. 
 
 ⑹ The Sun accounts for 99.86% of the mass in the solar system. 
 
 ⑺ There wouldn't be any life on Earth without the Sun. 
 
 ⑻ The sun is 93 million miles away from the Earth. 
 
 ⑼ Like the Earth, the sun has layers. But unlike the Earth, the sun is entirely gaseous; there is no solid surface. 
 
 ⑽ The temperature at the very centre of the Sun is about 27 million degrees Fahrenheit (F). 
 

⑴ Pluto is a dwarf planet in the Kuiper belt, a ring of bodies beyond the orbit of Neptune. 
 
 ⑵ Pluto is named after the Roman god of the underworld. 
 
 ⑶ Pluto was reclassified from a planet to a dwarf planet in 2006. 
 
 ⑷ Pluto was discovered on February 18th, 1930 by the Lowell Observatory. 
 
 ⑸ Pluto is one-third of water. 
 

⑴ The asteroid belt (sometimes referred to as the main asteroid belt) orbits between Mars and Jupiter. 
 
 ⑵ It contains a great many solid, irregularly shaped bodies, of many sizes but much smaller than planets, called asteroids or minor planets. 
 
 ⑶ Most asteroids are in a “belt”. 
 

// Below codes are For Terrestrial Quiz 1. How many planets are in the terrestrial planets? 2. How many days does Earth take to travel around the sun? 3. Which is the biggest Terrestrial planet? 4. Which planet is the hottest planet? 5. How much distance is between Mercury to Sun? 6. Which planet has the shortest orbit in the Terrestrial planet? 7. Which planet was formed approximately 4.54 billion years ago. 8. Which planet is the only planet that has life? 9. Which planet is the same size as Earth? 10. Terrestrial planets are made of which material?
2 3 4 5
Mercury Venus Earth Mars Quiz Jupiter Saturn uranus Neptune Sun pluto asteroid belt Continue
